status: DRAFT — NOT PUBLISHED — SME REVIEW REQUIRED — THIN SOURCE COVERAGE
note: DRAFT end-of-course assessment for GP-445 pilot (WS2b). This course carries IICRC CEC hours (4) and its live description/slug advertise the course without any assessment currently seeded — see GP-444 evidence. Structured identically to data/seed/floor-care-quizzes.json (courseSlug binding, quiz array, questions/options/correctIndex). SOURCE COVERAGE IS THIN: unlike the other two GP-445 pilot courses, there is no dedicated docx or lesson-body text file for 'level-2-mould-remediation' in this repo — only the WordPress export module-title outline (data/wordpress-export/courses.json) and the CEC/discipline record (data/seed/cec-professional-assignments.json, 4 CEC). This file deliberately holds FEWER questions (10, the low end of the 10-15 target for a 4-5 CEC course) because most module titles in the outline have no elaborated body text to write a defensible question from. Every question below is grounded either in the course's own WP module-title outline, in Australian WHS facts already verified in-repo for the same regulatory topics on other CARSI courses (cited by file), or in moisture-meter/containment technical facts verified in other CARSI structural-drying course text files (data/CARSI_Specialty_Courses_Collection.txt, data/water_damage_restoration_courses.txt) — NOT this course's own material, since none exists. DO NOT seed or publish until the founder (SME) has supplied or approved the missing module content — see docs/runbooks/sme-review-checklist.md, which lists every flagged gap for this course explicitly. sourceRef is a review aid only and is not consumed by the seed script.
sourceGaps: Introduction to Mould — outline title only, no elaborated body text in repo. SME must supply or confirm mould biology/growth-condition content before this module can be assessed.
Preparation for Mould Remediation - Level 2 — outline title only, no elaborated body text in repo.
The Framework of Health and Safety Regulations — outline title only; question below uses general Model WHS framework facts verified elsewhere in the CARSI corpus, not this course's own text.
Cleaning Chemicals — outline title only, no product/dilution detail in repo for this course specifically.
Biological Hazards — outline title only, no elaborated body text in repo for this course specifically (cf. Microbial.docx courses, which do have this detail but are different courses).
Initial Inspection - Level 2 — outline title only; no described inspection method or Level 2-specific criteria in repo.
Plant and Machinery — outline title only, no equipment list or spec detail in repo.
Emergency Situations / Emergency Documentation — outline titles only, no elaborated procedure in repo.

Mould Remediation Processes — outline title only; this is the course's core technical content and has NO elaborated source text in repo. This is the single largest gap — the founder must supply the actual remediation method/sequence before a defensible assessment of this module can be written.
Disposal Mould Course / Removing Waste — outline titles only, no waste-classification detail specific to mould in repo.
Upgrade to Level 3 - Mould Remediation — this is a marketing/upsell line in the outline, not course content; excluded from the assessment entirely.

Level 1 prerequisite content — the course description says it 'follows on from Level 1', but no Level 1 Mould Remediation docx or text source was found in repo either, so no Level-1-recap question could be grounded.

questions.questionText: This course states it follows on from an earlier mould remediation course. What does the live course description say this Level 2 course builds toward?
options: It replaces the need for any prior mould training
It follows on from Level 1 and provides a further look at mould remediation, providing extended skills for business requirements
It is a standalone course with no relationship to other mould courses
It is only intended for business owners, not technicians

questions.questionText: Which topic in this course's outline addresses working in high-temperature conditions such as full PPE in a contained mould-affected space?
options: Heat Exhaustion and Heat Stroke
Documentation
Finalise Tasks
Moisture Meters

questions.questionText: A pin-type moisture meter is used to check a mould-affected wall. Why must the correct material calibration setting be selected before taking a reading?
options: Calibration only affects the display brightness
Different materials have different resistance-to-moisture relationships — using the wrong material setting (e.g. a wood setting on gypsum) produces a meaningless reading
Calibration is a marketing feature and does not affect accuracy
Pin meters do not require calibration
questions.sourceRef: data/CARSI_Specialty_Courses_Collection.txt (moisture meter technical content, verified elsewhere in the CARSI corpus — NOT this course's own docx; flagged for SME confirmation that Level 2 teaches the same standard)

questions.questionText: Why is a non-invasive (pinless) moisture meter typically used before a pin-type meter when scanning a mould-affected area?
options: It is more accurate than a pin meter in every situation
It scans quickly to identify wet zones without penetrating the surface, with any elevated reading then confirmed using a pin meter
It eliminates the need for any further testing
It is only used outdoors
questions.sourceRef: data/CARSI_Specialty_Courses_Collection.txt (moisture meter technical content, verified elsewhere in the CARSI corpus — NOT this course's own docx; flagged for SME confirmation)

questions.questionText: Why is containment established under negative pressure when mould is present in the work area?
options: Negative pressure keeps the work area warmer
It ensures air movement through the containment boundary is inward, from clean areas into the contaminated zone, preventing spread of contamination outward
It is only a comfort measure for workers
Negative pressure is only relevant to water damage jobs, not mould
questions.sourceRef: data/CARSI_Specialty_Courses_Collection.txt (containment technical content, verified elsewhere in the CARSI corpus — NOT this course's own docx; flagged for SME confirmation that Level 2 teaches the same containment standard)

questions.questionText: This course's outline lists 'Containment' as a distinct topic. In an occupied residential property, what must be communicated to occupants before containment is established?
options: Nothing — containment can be set up without notice
The containment boundaries and access restrictions, ensuring essential facilities such as bathrooms and kitchen access are not eliminated
Only the expected completion date
Containment is never used in occupied properties
questions.sourceRef: data/CARSI_Specialty_Courses_Collection.txt (containment technical content, verified elsewhere in the CARSI corpus — NOT this course's own docx; flagged for SME confirmation)

questions.questionText: The course outline includes 'Job Safety and Environmental Analysis' as a topic. Based on the same JSEA requirement documented for other CARSI restoration courses, when must a JSEA be reviewed and updated during a job?
options: JSEAs are fixed once signed and are never updated
When site conditions change, additional contamination is discovered, or an incident/exposure occurs
Only at the very end of the job, for record-keeping
Only if requested by the client
questions.sourceRef: Cross-referenced against Microbial.docx JSEA content (Infectious Control for the Business Owner, Module 16) for the same JSEA update-trigger requirement — this course's own outline names 'Job Safety and Environmental Analysis' as a topic but has no elaborated body text; SME must confirm Level 2 teaches the same trigger list

questions.questionText: The course outline lists 'Safety Data Sheets' as a topic for handling cleaning chemicals used in mould remediation. Which SDS sections are the standard priority for a field worker before first using a new chemical?
options: Section 2 (hazard identification) and Section 8 (exposure controls and PPE)
Section 1 (identification) only
Section 16 (other information) only
There is no priority — all sections carry equal field relevance
questions.sourceRef: Cross-referenced against Microbial.docx SDS content (Microbe Clean Basic Understanding Course, Module 15) for the same SDS priority-section guidance — this course's own outline names 'Safety Data Sheets' as a topic but has no elaborated body text; SME must confirm Level 2 teaches the same standard

questions.questionText: The course outline lists 'Finalise Tasks' near the end of the module sequence. Based on the equivalent completion/handover step verified in other CARSI restoration course content, what should happen when a job cannot be completed to the agreed scope?
options: Leave the site quietly without informing anyone
Document the reason and escalate to the supervisor rather than inventing a completion note
Complete unrelated extra work to fill the time instead
Ignore the shortfall since the client was already informed verbally at the start
questions.sourceRef: Cross-referenced against data/seed/floor-care-quizzes.json completion/handover principle (same CARSI professional-conduct standard) — this course's own outline names 'Finalise Tasks' as a topic but has no elaborated body text; SME must confirm Level 2 teaches an equivalent handover procedure

questions.questionText: Which of the following is explicitly listed in this course's own module outline (as opposed to being inferred from another CARSI course)?
options: Moisture Meters
ATP Terminology
Donning and Doffing PPE
Bacteria and Modes of Transmission
questions.sourceRef: data/wordpress-export/courses.json — level-2-mould-remediation description (module outline includes 'Moisture Meters' verbatim)
